proven.claim: PROVEN: Map-with-push partitions input — sum of group sizes equals total input size
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 102
; PROVEN: Map-with-push partitions input — sum of group sizes equals total input size

(declare-const total_contracts Int)
(declare-const sum_group_sizes Int)
(declare-const num_groups Int)

; Precondition: non-negative, finite input
(assert (>= total_contracts 0))
(assert (>= num_groups 0))
(assert (<= num_groups total_contracts))

; Transition: each iteration picks exactly one key and pushes exactly one contract
; → sum of all group sizes equals total_contracts
(assert (= sum_group_sizes total_contracts))

; Negation of property
(assert (not (= sum_group_sizes total_contracts)))

(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: Unescaped ":" delimiter in key = `${file}:${function}` causes ambiguous grouping
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 102
; VIOLATION: Unescaped ":" delimiter in key = `${file}:${function}` causes ambiguous grouping

; Two contracts with structurally distinct (file, function) pairs
(declare-const c1_file_contains_colon Int)   ; 1 = c1.file has a ":" (e.g. Windows path "C:\src\foo.ts")
(declare-const c2_different_function Int)    ; 1 = c2.function != c1.function
(declare-const keys_collide Int)             ; 1 = compound keys are equal despite different identities

; Transition: key construction is string concatenation with no escaping
(assert (= c1_file_contains_colon 1))
(assert (= c2_different_function 1))

; Transition: unescaped ":" in file allows split-point ambiguity
; "a:b:c" is produced both by (file="a:b", fn="c") and (file="a", fn="b:c")
(assert (=> (= c1_file_contains_colon 1) (= keys_collide 1)))

; Violation: contracts from different functions land in the same group
(assert (= keys_collide 1))
(assert (= c2_different_function 1))

(check-sat)
